meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master Debugging: Project Management for AI Student Developers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how systematic project management and tools like Antigravity and Claude Code transform your development workflow today.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about staring at code; it's about systemizing your project. By integrating rigorous project management, modular architecture, and AI-assisted analysis using Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform chaotic bugs into manageable tasks. Stop guessing—start tracking, isolating, and solving with a professional engineering framework that guarantees cleaner production-ready code.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your application refuses to launch. You’ve spent hours hunting a phantom error, feeling the weight of mounting technical debt and the frustration of a project stalled. This isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of process. The transition from student hacker to professional engineer is marked by one fundamental shift: moving from 'making it work' to 'managing the system.'

We are going to rewire your workflow. By treating your codebase like a high-stakes engineering project, you’ll stop fighting the code and start orchestrating it. Using modern tools like Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you will build the professional resilience required to ship robust software. Let’s turn your 'can't debug' nightmare into a systematic, repeatable success story.

steps.title: Architectural Mapping
steps.description: Before touching code, map your dependencies to identify where the logic breaks. Visualization prevents scope creep and highlights hidden bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current repository structure and generate a dependency graph. Identify potential circular dependencies or weak interfaces causing runtime instability.

steps.title: Isolation via Modularization
steps.description: Break your monolith into testable units. When you isolate components, you limit the surface area of potential bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my current monolith into isolated modules. Focus on creating high-cohesion, low-coupling interfaces for the core logic layer.

steps.title: Automated Context Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to inject environment-specific context into your debugging sessions, ensuring the AI understands your local build environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a diagnostic suite that logs environment variables and state transitions before every critical function call.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --context ./env.config --task 'Implement diagnostic logging for state transitions'

steps.title: Regression Testing Strategy
steps.description: Implement automated tests that run every time you modify code. This ensures that 'fixing' one bug doesn't spawn three new ones.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for my identified problematic modules. Ensure 90% coverage for edge cases involving null pointers.

steps.title: Iterative Feedback Loops
steps.description: Use the AI to review your fixes before committing. This peer-review process catches logic errors before they reach your main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my latest code changes for potential side effects. Compare the current fix against the original architectural requirements.

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'Debug Journal' in your README to track which hypotheses you've already tested.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude Code by explaining the logic out loud before asking for a fix.
Adopt a 'Fail-Fast' approach by adding assertions to your code that crash early rather than allowing silent failures.
Prioritize fixing the root cause over patching symptoms to prevent technical debt accumulation.
Version control is your safety net; commit small, atomic changes so you can always revert to a working state.
content.next_steps: Once your project is stable, focus on CI/CD integration to automate your deployment pipeline, ensuring that every push is automatically verified by your new test suite.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the professional methodology required to conquer any codebase. Don't let bugs define your progress; let your process define your success. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
